concept.conceptName: Natriuretic peptide B | Blood | Chemistry - non-challenge
Natriuretic peptide B | Serum or Plasma | Chemistry - non-challenge
Natriuretic peptide B [Mass/volume] adjusted for eGFR in Serum or Plasma

en.name: B-type natriuretic peptide (BNP) in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context B-type natriuretic peptide (BNP) is a 32-amino-acid peptide hormone produced by ventricular cardiomyocytes. Plasma BNP rises in heart failure and is widely used to rule out the diagnosis in patients presenting with dyspnoea [1]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by immunoassay. Results are reported as a mass concentration in pg/mL [2]. **Note: BNP vs NT-proBNP.** ProBNP is cleaved into equimolar amounts of the biologically active **BNP** and the biologically inactive **NT-proBNP** [1]. In plasma, NT-proBNP concentrations are roughly 6 times higher than BNP because their clearance mechanisms differ [1]. The two are **not numerically interchangeable** and use different cut-offs. This concept set covers **BNP only** ; NT-proBNP is captured by the separate concept set "N-terminal pro-B-type natriuretic peptide (NT-proBNP) in blood". ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on two LOINC Hierarchy concepts (scoped to `Chemistry - non-challenge`), each expanded to include all their descendants. The set resolves to three LOINC "Lab Test" concepts: - *3011960 / 30934-4 — Natriuretic peptide B [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: most widely used. - *3052295 / 47092-2 — Natriuretic peptide B [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: SI form. - *3031569 / 42637-9 — Natriuretic peptide B [Mass/volume] in Blood (LOINC)* **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`pg/mL`** (concept 8845 — picogram per millilitre) for `[Mass/volume]` codes and **`pmol/L`** (concept 8729 — picomole per litre) for `[Moles/volume]` codes. The stoichiometric conversion factor is `1 pg/mL ≈ 0.288 pmol/L`, derived from the BNP-32 molecular weight of 3472 Da [1]. ## Excluded Concepts The following LOINC concept is deliberately excluded by name: - *1469998 / 107231-3 — Natriuretic peptide B [Mass/volume] adjusted for eGFR in Serum or Plasma*: derived measurement (eGFR-adjusted), not the raw BNP concentration. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports a BNP value: `3011960 / 30934-4 — Natriuretic peptide B [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma`. - **Source reports NT-proBNP**: do **not** map to this concept set. Use "N-terminal pro-B-type natriuretic peptide (NT-proBNP) in blood" instead. - **Do not confuse with**: NT-proBNP (separate concept set, different molecule and cut-offs), ANP/proANP (atrial natriuretic peptide, different analyte), CNP (C-type natriuretic peptide). ## References [1] Novack ML, Zubair M. Natriuretic Peptide B Type Test. In: *StatPearls*. Treasure Island (FL): StatPearls Publishing. Available: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK556136/ [2] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 30934-4 — Natriuretic peptide B [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma. Available: https://loinc.org/30934-4/
